title: BSP v5p3x save/restore register audit
trigger: Check whether BSP v5p3x suspend/resume save/restore names a special register missing from the live SDMMC0 IDMAC setup.
findings: BSP common save/restore covers GCTRL, CLKCR, TMOUT, WIDTH, FTRGL, FUNS, DBGC, DMAC, DLBA, and IMASK.
BSP v5p3x-specific save/restore covers DRV_DL, SAMP_DL, DS_DL, and SD_NTSR.
BSP restore also calls the ACMDA hook, which writes A12A=0.

The local diagnostic tree already dumps or writes the live setup registers relevant to the IDMAC path, including FTRGL, THLD, FUNS, DBGC, CSDC, DRV_DL, SAMP_DL, DS_DL, A12A, DMAC, DLBA, GCTRL, IDIE, CHDA, CBDA, CBCR, and BBCR.
Save/restore is suspend/resume bookkeeping. It does not introduce a probe-time or per-transfer descriptor-fetch precondition.

public_search.notes: Search still did not reveal a public archive hit or answer for the manually sent A733 SDMMC0 descriptor-fetch question.

reason: The v5p3x-specific registers are delay/status bookkeeping already visible in diagnostic dumps or unrelated to descriptor memory reachability. The remaining failure is before descriptor word 0 is consumed in a fresh live transfer, not after suspend/resume.
next: Do not test suspend/resume save/restore register writes as a standalone descriptor-fetch fix.
Keep waiting for a maintainer/list reply or a source-backed SDMMC0 firewall/security/master-permission bit.
